methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Dexterity, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Samir Menon, is valued at $1.65B post-money on $291M total raised. The valuation assessment draws from a comp universe of nine private robotics firms at the same stage and one public company, with peer valuations ranging from $300M to $10.0B across a five-company sample (1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Collaborative Robotics, Fairmat, and GrayMatter Robotics).

Two methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $1.65B last post-money valuation and applying a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies $3.1B. Peer-median methodology across the five comparable companies yields $500M, reflecting the wide dispersion in the robotics sector—the median sits well below Dexterity's current mark, though 1X Technologies trades at $10.0B and Bedrock Robotics at $1.8B. The peer range ($300M–$10.0B) underscores significant valuation variance within the cohort.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $425M (low) to $3.6B (high), with a median of $1.8B. Dexterity's current $1.65B valuation falls near the lower bound of this range, marginally below the triangulated median. Key uncertainties include the appropriate stage-progression multiplier for the company's specific risk profile and the relevance of the broad peer distribution, particularly the outsized valuation of 1X Technologies, which may reflect differentiated capital intensity or market positioning not directly comparable to Dexterity.
